Response 219983597

Fulton Schools of Engineering OSHA Training

Community Engagement (CE) - source indicates reciprocal or jointly beneficial collaboration.

Activity Basics

Question

Activity Description (Short)

In FY26 (July 2025–June 2026), the partnership between Arizona State University, the ASU Foundation, and the Blackstone Charitable Foundation advanced significantly through workforce development, entrepreneurship, and career-readiness initiatives that expanded access to experiential learning and...

Question

Activity Start Date / End Date

Start Date

2025-07-01 (inferred from FY26 survey window)

End Date

2026-06-30 (inferred from FY26 survey window)

Question

Public Service or Community Engagement

Community Engagement (CE) - source indicates reciprocal or jointly beneficial collaboration.

People And Units

Question

Units

  • GCA (survey abbreviation
  • full ASU unit name not specified in source)
  • Office (survey text
  • unit not otherwise specified)
  • OSHA Training Institute Education Center
  • Innovation Institute
  • ASU Foundation
  • Office of Research and Sponsored Projects Administration

Question

Programs/Initiatives

OSHA Training Institute Education Center

Question

Faculty/Staff

  • Jennie Denison (Director Global Corporate Advancement) - jennie.denison@asufoundation.org
  • Additional ASU collaborators from survey: This partnership has been highly collaborative across multiple ASU units and support functions. Key collaborators include Academic Alliances within the Office of the University Provost, which serves as a central lead for transfer pathways and workforce alignment initiatives, as well as the Ira A. Fulton Schools of Engineering and the OSHA Training Institute Education Center (OSHA Center), which support workforce training, safety certification programming, and skilled trades curriculum development. Additional collaboration has included the Entrepreneurship + Innovation Institute (Edson E+I), which led implementation of the Blackstone LaunchPad Summer Internship Pilot and coordinated employer engagement, student recruitment, internship placements, and career-readiness programming. The partnership also required significant coordination with institutional support and operational teams across ASU and the ASU Foundation, including: • ASUEP Media Relations • ASUF Writing Services • Gift Agreements • ASUF General Counsel • ASU General Counsel • ORSPA (Office of Research Sponsored Projects Administration) These teams supported proposal development, communications, legal review, agreement execution, sponsorship and gift structuring, compliance, and institutional coordination necessary to advance the partnership successfully.

Question
